stories.title: Model recruiter who introduced young women to Jeffrey Epstein found dead in France
stories.summary: French modeling scout Daniel Siad, 69, was found dead at his home in Colombes, a Paris suburb, on Monday. Authorities opened an investigation and ordered an autopsy to determine the cause of death. Siad had been under scrutiny for allegedly connecting young women to Jeffrey Epstein over a decade. French prosecutors confirmed no foul play has been ruled out yet.

stories.title: Argentina Vs Spain World Cup Final Analysis | The myth surrounding Argentina’s victimhood
stories.summary: Spain won the 2026 FIFA World Cup final 1-0 over Argentina in a commanding performance. The match capped record-breaking attendance and viewership for the tournament hosted in the U.S. Messi’s popularity in the UK dropped sharply after Argentina’s controversial conduct, including post-match altercations and political displays. FIFA’s disciplinary committee will review incidents from the final as the tournament concludes.

stories.title: National Guard deploys as flash floods in West Virginia inundate homes and businesses
stories.summary: West Virginia declared a state of emergency for all 55 counties after torrential rains triggered flash floods, washing out roads and submerging homes and businesses. Governor Patrick Morrisey deployed 310 National Guard members and 19 helicopters for rescues, with no deaths or missing reported as of Wednesday. Over 85 water rescues were conducted by state agencies. Recovery efforts continue as officials assess damage and restore access to cut-off areas.

stories.title: Alejandro Garnacho completes Chelsea FC exit as Aston Villa transfer terms revealed
stories.summary: Alejandro Garnacho will join Aston Villa on a one-year loan with a conditional permanent transfer next summer. The 22-year-old Argentine winger, who struggled at Chelsea with just 12 goal contributions in 43 appearances, has already completed a medical. Villa manager Unai Emery pushed for the deal, which includes a four-year contract if conditions are met. Chelsea set their asking price at £43m in June and are satisfied with the terms.

stories.title: Fernandes off to a flyer against MK Dons
stories.summary: Mateus Fernandes scored in the third minute of his Tottenham debut, helping Spurs beat MK Dons 1-0 in a pre-season friendly at Hotspur Way on July 22, 2026. The £85m midfielder’s first-time volley off a Mathys Tel corner sealed the win. Sandro Tonali also debuted alongside Fernandes in midfield. Spurs now head to New Zealand and Australia for their next tour.

stories.title: Microsoft launches Xbox backward compatibility for PC
stories.summary: Microsoft launched Xbox backward compatibility for PC today, starting with four original Xbox games: Blinx: The Time Sweeper, Conker: Live and Reloaded, Crimson Skies: High Road to Revenge, and Fusion Frenzy. The titles are available for purchase or included with Xbox Game Pass, with digital licenses transferring from console to PC. Enhanced features include 4x resolution upscaling and adjustable graphics settings. Microsoft plans to expand the lineup and add features like achievements later this year.

stories.title: Tottenham 1-0 MK Dons: Mateus Fernandes marks debut with screamer as Spurs open pre-season with a win
stories.summary: Tottenham opened their pre-season with a 1-0 win over MK Dons at Hotspur Way on July 22, 2026. New signing Mateus Fernandes scored the only goal in the third minute with a first-time volley. Sandro Tonali and Martin Dubravka also made their debuts in the behind-closed-doors friendly. Spurs will continue pre-season preparations to build chemistry ahead of the new campaign.

stories.title: How Crysencio Summerville's 'crazy 48 hours' unfolded as medical finally authorised for West Ham exit
stories.summary: Crysencio Summerville’s move to Saudi Pro League side Al-Hilal is set to be completed after West Ham accepted a £55 million fee with potential add-ons pushing the deal to £65 million. The 24-year-old Dutch winger has given the green light and will undergo a medical ahead of the transfer. West Ham’s total income from player exits this summer now exceeds £150 million following earlier sales. The move caps a frenetic 48 hours involving competing bids from AS Roma before Al-Hilal secured the deal.

stories.title: Kate Middleton se confie sur ce passe-temps qui l’a aidée à « s’évader » durant son combat contre le cancer
stories.summary: Kate Middleton a partagé son expérience de lutte contre le cancer, soulignant l'importance de maintenir des activités qui apportent du plaisir pendant la maladie. La princesse de Galles a notamment mentionné les difficultés cognitives qu'elle a rencontrées pendant son traitement, notamment la fatigue et la baisse de l'attention. Elle a également visité des patients à l'hôpital Christie pour leur apporter soutien et évoqué son propre combat contre le cancer. Kate Middleton a repris ses activités et engagements royaux après sa rémission en 2025.

stories.title: Forte dei Marmi, rissa in pieno centro nel locale storico: volano sedie e tavolini
stories.summary: A violent brawl erupted in Forte dei Marmi’s historic Caffè Morin around 1:30 a.m. Saturday, with chairs, tables and even a bicycle hurled in the chaos. Dozens of young people—many reportedly minors—clashed in the upscale Versilia resort, captured on phones but not reported to police. Authorities arrived to disperse the crowd as bystanders fled. Local officials cite rising late-night disturbances along viale Morin this summer.

stories.title: Soap legend with 'huge talent' returning to Coronation Street after 20 years
stories.summary: Coronation Street is welcoming back actress Elizabeth Rider, 68, after a 20-year absence. She will play a new role on the show this summer, marking her fourth appearance on the ITV soap. Rider, a legendary actress with a huge amount of talent, has had a long and impressive career in British television, including roles in EastEnders, Emmerdale, and Hollyoaks. Her return is expected to bring excitement to the show's upcoming storylines.

stories.title: Boyd found guilty of murdering Mark Carroll in Finglas
stories.summary: Derek Boyd, 31, was convicted of murdering Mark Carroll in Finglas, Dublin, on Wednesday after a trial at the Central Criminal Court. Boyd fatally stabbed Carroll in June 2024 during a row at his home while serving a suspended sentence for manslaughter. He will be sentenced on Tuesday to life in prison. Boyd fled to Scotland hours after the killing but was later arrested and extradited.

stories.title: Ed Harris Felt ‘Misled’ by ‘Dutton Ranch’ About ‘Inconsequential’ Role: ‘Get Me the F— Out of Here’ (EXCLUSIVE)
stories.summary: Veteran actor Ed Harris felt misled about his role on the "Yellowstone" spinoff "Dutton Ranch," stating he was told he'd be one of four main characters but felt underused and inconsequential. Harris expressed his discontent, saying "get me the f— out of here" halfway through filming Season 1. He's signed on for two seasons, with filming for Season 2 set to start in February 2027. Harris was reportedly told his character will have more to do in Season 2.

stories.title: Lucía Pombo y Álvaro López-Huertas ya son padres de su primera hija
stories.summary: Lucía Pombo and Álvaro López-Huertas have welcomed their first child, a daughter named Lola Belén, on July 21. The couple, who met through their parents' friendship, had been expecting the arrival of their baby after announcing the pregnancy in April. This marks a new chapter for the pair as they begin their journey as parents.

stories.title: Jamel Debbouze, comblé par Mélissa : "ne change absolument rien"
stories.summary: Jamel Debbouze celebrated his wife Mélissa Theuriau’s 48th birthday on July 18 with a heartfelt Instagram post. The couple, married since 2008, has two children. Debbouze wrote, "Continue to shine, make others shine, and light up our lives." The post, featuring a tender photo of the pair, resonated widely online.

stories.title: Reynolds and Mac share Wrexham's European excitement
stories.summary: Wrexham Women's team will make their European debut on Wednesday at the historic Racecourse Stadium, hosting Armenian side FC Pyunik in a Women's Champions League first qualifying round semi-final. This marks the first time Wrexham will play European football in 30 years, following the club's rapid rise under Hollywood owners Ryan Reynolds and Rob Mac. The team's head coach, Jenny Sugarman, says the owners are "super excited" for the team's progress. The match will be a significant step in Wrexham's ambition to become one of the world's top women's teams.

stories.title: Mercato : accord en vue entre l'AS Monaco et le FC Nantes pour Matthis Abline
stories.summary: AS Monaco is close to signing French forward Matthis Abline from FC Nantes for a record-breaking 30 million euros. Abline, 23, has already agreed to join the Principality. The transfer would be a significant deal for both clubs, with Abline set to become the most expensive player in FC Nantes' history. The deal is expected to be finalized soon, marking a major departure for FC Nantes after a season of relegation struggles.

stories.title: France pesticide fight : Macron rejects Monique Barbut’s resignation as farm law opens disputed exemptions
stories.summary: France’s President Emmanuel Macron rejected Environment Minister Monique Barbut’s resignation on July 22, 2026, after she opposed a new farm law allowing disputed pesticide exemptions. Parliament had passed the emergency agricultural legislation the day before, sparking her protest over reauthorizing acetamiprid and flupyradifurone. Barbut, who called the move an environmental setback, remains in post despite her resignation. The government now faces continued debate over pesticide policy and ecological standards.

stories.title: MLS schedule Wednesday, July 22: Will Lewandowski debut? Is Messi available?
stories.summary: Major League Soccer resumed its season Wednesday, July 22, with a full 15-match slate after a World Cup break. Inter Miami hosts Chicago Fire at 7:30 p.m. ET, with Robert Lewandowski’s MLS debut possible and Lionel Messi’s availability uncertain. All games stream on Apple TV, with Saturday’s 15 matches leading to the July 29 All-Star Game against Mexico’s Liga MX.

stories.title: Harry Potter keert voorlopig voor het laatst terug naar het grote doek: Pathé viert 25-jarig jubileum met 24-uurs marathon
stories.summary: Harry Potter films return to Dutch cinemas for a 25th anniversary celebration. From August 26 to September 2, Pathé will screen the first film at a reduced Classic rate and host a 24-hour marathon on August 29-30, showing all eight films. This is reportedly the last time the series will be shown in Dutch cinemas. Fans can purchase tickets starting July 31, Harry Potter's birthday.

stories.title: Violenta lite a Bari Sardo, ucciso un giovane
stories.summary: A 19-year-old man, Simone Concas, was killed in a violent altercation in Bari Sardo, Italy. He was stabbed repeatedly by the driver of a car after a dispute near a sports field at around 6 pm. A 25-year-old woman who was with him was also injured and taken to hospital in Cagliari. The incident is under investigation.

stories.title: Gatti sforzeschi, l'appello di Feltri al Tg4: "Ascoltate questi volontari. Andate loro incontro"
stories.summary: Eighteen cats at the Castello Sforzesco in Milan are in need of accessible entrances to receive daily care. The cats' colony has gained public attention through a campaign promoted by a local newspaper and supported by a television program. The city's mayor is being asked to find a solution to allow volunteers to continue assisting the cats. The situation is expected to be addressed further in the coming days.

stories.title: ‘The doctors will decide’ – Adam Yates may not continue in Tour de France due to stomach infection
stories.summary: British cyclist Adam Yates is struggling with a stomach infection at the 2026 Tour de France, finishing stage 17 over 26 minutes behind the winner. His participation in the final stages is uncertain, with crucial support needed for race leader Tadej Pogačar in the high mountains. Yates's condition must improve significantly for him to be of assistance. His team will decide on his continued participation in the coming days.

stories.title: Hoje pode ver em direto na TV…
stories.summary: Tonight’s live sports lineup includes SC Braga facing Zeleznicar Pancevo in the UEFA Conference League second qualifying round at 20:00. Levski Sofia and Universitatea Craiova kick off the Champions League second leg at 18:30. The Tour de France rolls on with Stage 17 from Chambéry to Voiron starting at 12:15. Brazilian league action continues with Coritiba against Palmeiras at 23:30.

stories.title: Spanish singer Rosalía sparks outrage in Argentina
stories.summary: Spanish singer Rosalía faced backlash in Argentina after a performance in Buenos Aires on March 15, where she wore a dress resembling a traditional Catholic garment. The controversy erupted when images of her outfit sparked accusations of cultural appropriation. Authorities and religious groups condemned the attire as disrespectful. The incident has drawn widespread attention on social media.

stories.title: Jasmine Crockett 'finds a racism underbelly in every single place': Martha MacCalum
stories.summary: Congresswoman Jasmine Crockett testified Tuesday at a House Oversight hearing on diversity, equity and inclusion in law schools, calling out what she described as systemic racism in legal education. The hearing, held in Washington, D.C., drew attention after Crockett’s remarks about encountering racial bias in multiple institutions. Lawmakers are expected to review testimony and consider policy recommendations in the coming weeks.

stories.title: Philipsen wins stage 17 and reopens a battle for the green jersey that seemed closed
stories.summary: Jasper Philipsen won stage 17 of the Tour de France 2026, a 174.7 kilometer race between Chambéry and Voiron. The Belgian rider from Alpecin-Premier Tech took the victory, with Mauro Schmid second and Olav Kooij third. Philipsen's win reopens the battle for the green jersey, closing in on leader Mads Pedersen. The favorites in the general classification remain unchanged, now facing the decisive alpine triptych.

stories.title: Dream School maakt doorstart met nieuwe gezichten
stories.summary: Dream School krijgt een doorstart met een nieuwe schoolleiding. Voetbalscheidsrechter Danny Makkelie wordt rector en Ouassima Tajmout mentor, vervangend Eric van ’t Zelfde en Lucia Rijker na zeven seizoenen. Het achtste seizoen verschijnt in 2027 op televisie. De nieuwe gezichten moeten jongeren opnieuw inspireren.

stories.title: Max Wöber leaves Leeds United
stories.summary: Austrian defender Max Wöber has left Leeds United after the club and player agreed to mutually terminate his contract. Wöber joined Leeds in 2023 from RB Salzburg and made 19 appearances in his debut season. He scored one goal in eight league appearances for the club in the 2024/25 season. Wöber now departs Elland Road on a permanent basis.

stories.title: Delmastro: the Executive Committee has rejected the prosecutors’ request to obtain the chat records. The M5S occupies the government benches
stories.summary: The Senate’s Committee on Authorisation to Proceed blocked prosecutors from accessing chat records between Deputy Andrea Delmastro and a detained restaurateur linked to mafia charges. Five Star Movement senators protested on the government benches, chanting against the decision, while centre-right MPs supported the refusal. The committee cited vague requests and lack of less intrusive alternatives. Prosecutors may now seek alternative legal avenues.

stories.title: Car plows into crowd in Modena, killing 55-year-old woman after two months of agony
stories.summary: A 55-year-old woman died today after being struck by a car in Modena’s city center two months ago. Monica Esposito, hit on May 16 while in a crowd, suffered critical injuries and never regained consciousness despite treatment. The driver, Salim El Koudri, remains under investigation as authorities assess the incident. Her husband, also injured in the crash, is recovering in a rehabilitation facility.

stories.title: Vijftien gewonden bij botsing trams op Erasmusbrug in Rotterdam, 'vreselijk ongeluk'
stories.summary: Two trams collided on Rotterdam’s Erasmus Bridge around 11:10 a.m., injuring 15 people, one seriously. Emergency crews freed a trapped passenger and treated others on-site or at hospitals. The crash damaged both trams and closed the bridge to all traffic. Authorities later adjusted the injury count from an earlier higher figure.

stories.title: Fakir Case: Lawyer Slams Authorities for Blaming Moroccan Man’s Murder on His Character
stories.summary: Italian police fatally shot Moroccan man Abderrahim Fakir outside Bologna on Sunday. Bologna-based asylum lawyer Barbara Spinelli condemned attempts to blame Fakir, calling him a worker who struggled after laying off employees due to declining business. Spinelli, who handled his residence permit renewal last year, emphasized his character over speculation. Authorities have not released further details on the shooting.

stories.title: Foyle Cup: Record-breaking tournament kicks off
stories.summary: A record 1,013 teams are participating in the largest Foyle Cup in the tournament's 34-year history, with almost 4,000 matches scheduled across over 60 venues in the north west. The six-day tournament kicked off on Monday with a traditional opening parade in Londonderry. Thousands of young footballers from Europe and North America are taking part. The event is expected to conclude on Saturday.

stories.title: Scottish Labour’s rivals decry ‘chancer’ Anas Sarwar for resigning to join Andy Burnham’s government
stories.summary: Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar resigned to join Andy Burnham’s government as a peer and trade minister. Rivals in Holyrood accused him of hypocrisy for accepting a House of Lords seat after previously vowing to abolish it. The SNP demanded he donate his salary to charity, calling his move unacceptable. Labour has not commented on the criticism.

stories.title: ‘He knows what he’s doing’: Burnham’s off-the-cuff remarks inspire headlines and headaches
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Andy Burnham is facing controversy over his handling of policy changes, including a possible income tax threshold increase, just three days into his tenure. He has been accused of performing U-turns on key issues, including taxes for higher earners and private sector rents. The income tax threshold change is a major point of contention, with reports suggesting it may be raised. Burnham's unscripted comments are sparking headaches for his administration.

stories.title: FCC Chair Says ABC’s Decision to Not Broadcast Trump Speech Live Will Be Part of Agency’s Review of Station Licenses
stories.summary: FCC Chair Brendan Carr says ABC’s decision to skip live coverage of Donald Trump’s July 16 speech on election security will be reviewed as part of the agency’s early license checks for eight ABC-owned stations. Trump claimed during the speech that China illegally accessed millions of voter files to manipulate U.S. elections in 2018 and 2020. Carr, a Trump appointee, has previously argued broadcasters may be required to air presidential remarks live. The FCC’s review could determine whether the stations face penalties or license adjustments.

stories.title: Rangers to fight McInnes ban for 'disgusting' penalty remark
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been handed a four-match touchline ban by the Scottish FA for calling a late penalty decision "disgusting" in May. The penalty, awarded to Celtic's Kelechi Iheanacho, was a stoppage-time spot-kick that kept Celtic one point behind Hearts, who ultimately won the title. McInnes will contest the ban, stating the remarks were directed at the decision, not officials. He will be in the stands as Rangers open the new Scottish Premiership season.

stories.title: McInnes given four-match ban for 'disgusting' Celtic penalty remark
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been given a four-match ban by the Scottish FA for his "disgusting" comments about a late penalty awarded to Celtic at Motherwell in May. The penalty, converted by Kelechi Iheanacho, helped Celtic stay one point behind Hearts, who ultimately won the title. McInnes will serve the ban, with one match suspended until the end of the season. He will be in the stands as Rangers open their Scottish Premiership campaign.

stories.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
stories.summary: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein, Daniel Siad, 69, was found dead at his home in Colombes, a Paris suburb, on Monday. An investigation into his death has been opened, with a post-mortem examination to be carried out. Siad's name appeared thousands of times in Epstein files, and he had been under investigation for organised human trafficking and conspiracy charges since February. The investigation will continue to identify all individuals potentially involved.

stories.title: European Commission Approves Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger
stories.summary: The European Commission approved the $111 billion merger between Paramount Skydance and Warner Bros. Discovery on Wednesday, removing a major hurdle for the deal. As a condition, Paramount must end its European film distribution partnership with Universal within 13 months. A U.S. federal judge temporarily paused the merger on Monday following a request from 12 states citing competition concerns. The commission concluded the deal won’t harm competition in TV or film production, though it flagged film distribution concentration risks.

stories.title: Sophie Thatcher Is Ready to Move Beyond ‘Yellowjackets’ and Step Out of the Darkness
stories.summary: Sophie Thatcher, known for her dark roles in Yellowjackets and Heretic, is shifting gears with a new project. The 22-year-old actor, raised in a Mormon household, is stepping away from intense characters to explore lighter roles. Her next film, Companion, marks a departure from her previous work. Critics expect her to bring fresh energy to upcoming projects.

stories.title: | Comunicado Oficial | Información LVII Trofeo Colombino
stories.summary: Thousands of fans are expected to attend the LVII Trofeo Colombino at the Nuevo Colombino stadium in Huelva, Spain on Wednesday, prompting organizers to advise early arrival due to high expected attendance. The majority of parking lots near the stadium are closed for Fiestas Colombinas setup, except for a designated area near Gol Norte accessible from Avenida del Decano. Fans driving to the stadium should be prepared for potential traffic congestion. The event is set to take place on July 21, 2026.

stories.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
stories.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after signing Brazilian midfielder Casemiro. The league confirmed the probe Wednesday, the same day Miami announced his three-year deal with an option to extend through June 2029. Inter Miami claims it acquired Casemiro’s discovery rights from the LA Galaxy, though the league has not yet released settlement terms. The investigation focuses on whether Miami followed MLS rules in securing his rights.

stories.title: Robotic Servicing Mission Launches with NASA Support
stories.summary: A robotic spacecraft, the Mission Robotic Vehicle, has launched from Cape Canaveral on a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket and is now en route to geosynchronous Earth orbit. The NASA-supported Robotic Servicing of Geosynchronous Satellites payload will use its advanced robotics to service spacecraft, extending the life of existing satellites by installing propulsion modules. Funded by DARPA, the mission aims to advance US capabilities for in-space servicing and assembly. The spacecraft is expected to begin inspecting and upgrading satellites in orbit.

stories.title: Mamdani backs off pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
stories.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has backed off his pledge to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, citing a lack of authority to do so. Mamdani now calls on US officials to act on the International Criminal Court's arrest warrant for Netanyahu over alleged war crimes. The US is not a member of the ICC, which would allow for Netanyahu's arrest in an ICC member state. Mamdani's reversal comes after President Donald Trump stated Netanyahu would not be arrested in the US.

stories.title: €1.5 billion for tax cuts in Budget - Economic Statement
stories.summary: The Irish government's Summer Economic Statement reveals €1.5 billion will be allocated for tax cuts in the upcoming Budget, set for October. This is part of a €8.5 billion package that includes €7 billion in spending increases, bringing total expenditure to €125 billion in 2027. The tax cuts are aimed at allowing workers to keep more of their pay, with the government committed to personal taxation changes. The statement is being published by Minister for Finance Simon Harris and Minister for Public Expenditure Jack Chambers.

stories.title: Bohs look to lay Dalymount platform against Ballkani
stories.summary: Bohemians face Ballkani in the Conference League second round qualifier at Dalymount Park. Bohemians aim to build on their first European hurdle success, having secured a minimum of €700,000 in prize money. They previously defeated St Joseph's 2-0 and drew goalless in the first qualifying round. The winner will advance to the next round, with a sterner test expected against Ballkani.

stories.title: Aston Martin secures £550m loan deal
stories.summary: Aston Martin has secured a £550m loan deal to bolster its finances. The luxury car maker, headquartered in Gaydon, Warwickshire, has been struggling with net losses of £493.2m last year, a 50% increase. The loan will fund future product plans and bolster its balance sheet. The company's half-year results are set to be published on 29 July.

stories.title: Delmastro, Vannacci e la fine della questione morale in Italia
stories.summary: Italian politician Delmastro has been granted parliamentary immunity, blocking magistrates from investigating his business dealings. The move has sparked widespread criticism and protests, with a demonstration in Bologna two days ago. The immunity will hinder investigations into Delmastro's family business and a high-profile case involving a former justice minister. The outcome is likely to fuel further controversy and public outcry.

stories.title: Man Utd news: £60m transfer target makes decision as star gets 'calls and texts' over move
stories.summary: Manchester United striker Joshua Zirkzee is reportedly a transfer target for Italian club Juventus. The Dutch forward has struggled to make an impact in the Premier League since joining United in 2024 for £36.5 million. Zirkzee has scored just five goals in 56 Premier League appearances. Juventus is considering a move for the 23-year-old forward.

stories.title: 1win y FUTTO establecen el récord mundial oficial del partido de fútbol disputado a mayor altura sobre un edificio
stories.summary: A record-breaking 3-on-3 soccer match was held at 196 meters above Bogotá, Colombia, on a helipad atop the Torre Colpatria building. The event, organized by 1win and FUTTO, was certified by World Record Certification as the highest soccer match ever played. Six Colombian soccer influencers participated in the match, which was part of a larger celebration of soccer culture. The event's success is expected to be followed by more innovative soccer experiences.

stories.title: VNL Finals: Brasile-Giappone 3-1, l’Italia incrocia le verdeoro in semifinale
stories.summary: In the VNL Finals, Brazil defeated Japan 3-1, advancing to the semifinals where they will face Italy on Saturday, July 25, at the East Asian Games in Macao. Italy had previously defeated the Netherlands 3-0 and will be looking to avenge their previous loss to Brazil in the first week of the intercontinental phase. The winner of the Italy-Brazil match will face the winner of the Turkey-Canada or USA-China match in the final.
stories.trend: nazionale femminile di pallavolo del brasile

stories.title: Natalie Fleet leaves 'triggering' safeguarding minister role
stories.summary: Labour MP Natalie Fleet resigned as safeguarding minister on 12 May this year after finding the role overwhelmingly triggering. The former victim of grooming and rape cited the job’s exposure to harrowing cases as making her ill. She announced her departure on X, calling it the bravest decision she had made. Fleet will support her successor in the role.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A parliamentary report urges Britain to target ultra-processed foods to cut obesity rates. MPs highlight sugar and refined carbs as key drivers, not fat, citing health risks like type 2 diabetes. Current policies still focus on fat reduction, pushing manufacturers to replace it with sweeteners. The call comes as obesity costs the NHS billions annually, with reformulation targets set for next year.

stories.title: The Walking Dead: Dead City Season 3 Is a Welcome Sort-of-Reboot That Puts Maggie and Negan on the Right Road
stories.summary: The Walking Dead: Dead City Season 3 premieres Sunday, July 26 at 9pm ET/PT on AMC and AMC+, marking a new direction for the series with Maggie and Negan as frenemies-to-friends. The third season delivers compelling new elements and a bold new direction, a welcome change from previous seasons. With a messy premiere episode, the series finds its footing in the back half, culminating in a standout episode. The new path for the characters is a promising start to the season.

stories.title: Las redes sociales de À Punt superan el millón y medio de seguidores
stories.summary: À Punt's social media presence has surpassed 1.5 million followers across various platforms. The public Valencian television network's profiles on Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook have grown by 32% over the past year, with a total increase of over 230,000 followers. À Punt Mèdia's Facebook channel leads with over 269,000 followers, followed by its Instagram channel with 228,000. This growth is accompanied by a 45% increase in digital content production in the first half of 2026.

stories.title: Israel may equip IDF with anti-submarine aircraft as tensions with Turkey grow
stories.summary: Israel is considering purchasing Boeing P-8 Poseidon anti-submarine aircraft for the IDF amid rising tensions with Turkey. The P-8 Poseidon, based on a converted commercial jet, has a 2,200-kilometer operational range and can detect diesel submarines using advanced sensors. The aircraft carries a nine-member crew and can carry torpedoes, bombs, and naval mines. The IDF currently uses the Oron for strategic intelligence and surveillance.

stories.title: Garnacho, Rogers and two clubs trying to balance the books
stories.summary: Chelsea's financial woes persist despite a record £300m in player sales last season, with the club still carrying significant debts. They were fined £2.6m by Uefa for breaching financial regulations, but can write off £1.7m if they meet targets by next summer. Chelsea's squad value is £1.3bn, second only to Manchester City in the Premier League. The club will continue to navigate Uefa's financial rules as they balance their books.

stories.title: Dublin Airport is about to boom. Why is Aer Lingus shrinking itself?
stories.summary: Aer Lingus is cutting 7.7% of its flights and staff, despite Dublin Airport's passenger cap being lifted and charges reduced. This move comes as the airline's parent company, International Airlines Group, prioritizes operating margins across its subsidiaries. IAG's focus on 12-15% margins determines investment allocation, with airlines meeting this target receiving funding for new planes and routes. The decision may raise questions about Aer Lingus' place within IAG, following a €1.4 billion acquisition in 2015.

stories.title: Başakşehir vs Inter Turku match lineup | UEFA Europa Conference League | Qualification · 22 Jul 2026
stories.summary: Başakşehir and Inter Turku faced off in the UEFA Europa Conference League Qualification on Wednesday, with Başakşehir leading in a 4-4-2 formation under head coach Nuri Şahin. The match was played at Basaksehir Fatih Terim Stadyumu. The teams' lineups and formations were key to the match, with Inter Turku setting up in a 4-3-3. The outcome of the match is not specified in the provided reports.

stories.title: Daniel Siad, Model Scout Who Connected Women to Epstein, Is Found Dead in France
stories.summary: Daniel Siad, who was found dead on Monday, repeatedly introduced Jeffrey Epstein to young women, according to messages he exchanged with Mr. Epstein. Mr. Siad had denied wrongdoing.

stories.title: Lawsuit Against Meta Over Social Media Addiction Is Dropped
stories.summary: For Meta, the move was a reprieve after it and YouTube were found guilty in another case in March of negligence and personal injury for their platforms’ addictive features.

stories.title: Hitler’s Birth Place in Austria Is Now a Police Station
stories.summary: The government repurposed the 17th century building in hopes of preventing the site from becoming an attraction for Nazi sympathizers.

stories.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
stories.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

stories.title: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
stories.summary: Two Russians were sentenced to 11 and eight years in prison in Angola’s capital, Luanda, for terrorism and spying. Igor Ratchin and Lev Lakshtanov were convicted over alleged efforts to incite protests, spread disinformation and interfere in next year’s presidential election. Authorities linked them to Africa Politology, described as a Russian political network tied to the Wagner group. Both denied involvement and charges.

stories.title: Geld versteckt: Böhmermann-Zuschauer haben "die Möglichkeit, richtig reich zu werden"
stories.summary: Jan Böhmermann has hidden 900 euros in nine secret locations in and around Kassel, Germany. Viewers of his ZDF show "Lass dich überwachen!" have the chance to find the money. The locations are revealed in a recent episode featuring a local resident and geocacher, Bernd.

stories.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
stories.summary: A woman in France has been formally charged in the 2024 stabbing death of British-South African mother Karen Carter, 65, in the village of Trémolat. The 70-year-old suspect was initially held in 2025 before release and now faces judicial inquiry after prosecutors cited new evidence. Carter, who managed holiday rentals, was found near her car with fatal wounds. Authorities continue investigating the motive as her grieving husband describes the family’s shock.

stories.title: The PSOE backs Zapatero and leaves his future in the hands of Justice: "The truth will come to light"
stories.summary: The PSOE reaffirmed its full support for former Spanish Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero on Wednesday amid new allegations tied to the Plus Ultra rescue operation. The party called for the ongoing judicial investigation to clarify the facts, stating no reason exists to doubt Zapatero’s account. PSOE spokesperson Montse Mínguez emphasized that courts will determine the truth as the case remains in the investigative phase. The party maintained its confidence in Zapatero, asserting all his actions were lawful.

stories.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
stories.summary: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

stories.title: Obituary for David J. Ascione
stories.summary: David J. Ascione, known as D.J., died July 19, 2026, at 62 in Toms River, New Jersey. A lifelong resident, he worked at Ascione Florist and Greenhouse on Route 9 and served with the Pleasant Plains Volunteer Fire Company. He is survived by his mother, sister, aunt, uncle, and extended family across New Jersey and Arizona. Funeral services will be private, with flowers or memorial trees accepted at the family’s floral shop.
stories.sourceName: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals
sources: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals

stories.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Hacked Into Hugging Face, a Digital Library
stories.summary: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.

stories.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
stories.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City’s legionnaires’ disease outbreak, bringing the toll to five fatalities among 82 confirmed cases. Health officials traced the outbreak to cooling towers in Manhattan’s Upper East Side, where 34 tested positive for legionella bacteria. No new cases have been reported in six days, but officials warn figures may change as testing continues. Remaining cooling tower results are expected within a week.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

offbeat.title: Model recruiter who introduced young women to Jeffrey Epstein found dead in France
offbeat.summary: French modeling scout Daniel Siad, 69, was found dead at his home in Colombes, a Paris suburb, on Monday. Authorities opened an investigation and ordered an autopsy to determine the cause of death. Siad had been under scrutiny for allegedly connecting young women to Jeffrey Epstein over a decade. French prosecutors confirmed no foul play has been ruled out yet.

offbeat.title: Argentina Vs Spain World Cup Final Analysis | The myth surrounding Argentina’s victimhood
offbeat.summary: Spain won the 2026 FIFA World Cup final 1-0 over Argentina in a commanding performance. The match capped record-breaking attendance and viewership for the tournament hosted in the U.S. Messi’s popularity in the UK dropped sharply after Argentina’s controversial conduct, including post-match altercations and political displays. FIFA’s disciplinary committee will review incidents from the final as the tournament concludes.

offbeat.title: National Guard deploys as flash floods in West Virginia inundate homes and businesses
offbeat.summary: West Virginia declared a state of emergency for all 55 counties after torrential rains triggered flash floods, washing out roads and submerging homes and businesses. Governor Patrick Morrisey deployed 310 National Guard members and 19 helicopters for rescues, with no deaths or missing reported as of Wednesday. Over 85 water rescues were conducted by state agencies. Recovery efforts continue as officials assess damage and restore access to cut-off areas.

offbeat.title: Alejandro Garnacho completes Chelsea FC exit as Aston Villa transfer terms revealed
offbeat.summary: Alejandro Garnacho will join Aston Villa on a one-year loan with a conditional permanent transfer next summer. The 22-year-old Argentine winger, who struggled at Chelsea with just 12 goal contributions in 43 appearances, has already completed a medical. Villa manager Unai Emery pushed for the deal, which includes a four-year contract if conditions are met. Chelsea set their asking price at £43m in June and are satisfied with the terms.

offbeat.title: Fernandes off to a flyer against MK Dons
offbeat.summary: Mateus Fernandes scored in the third minute of his Tottenham debut, helping Spurs beat MK Dons 1-0 in a pre-season friendly at Hotspur Way on July 22, 2026. The £85m midfielder’s first-time volley off a Mathys Tel corner sealed the win. Sandro Tonali also debuted alongside Fernandes in midfield. Spurs now head to New Zealand and Australia for their next tour.

offbeat.title: Microsoft launches Xbox backward compatibility for PC
offbeat.summary: Microsoft launched Xbox backward compatibility for PC today, starting with four original Xbox games: Blinx: The Time Sweeper, Conker: Live and Reloaded, Crimson Skies: High Road to Revenge, and Fusion Frenzy. The titles are available for purchase or included with Xbox Game Pass, with digital licenses transferring from console to PC. Enhanced features include 4x resolution upscaling and adjustable graphics settings. Microsoft plans to expand the lineup and add features like achievements later this year.

offbeat.title: Tottenham 1-0 MK Dons: Mateus Fernandes marks debut with screamer as Spurs open pre-season with a win
offbeat.summary: Tottenham opened their pre-season with a 1-0 win over MK Dons at Hotspur Way on July 22, 2026. New signing Mateus Fernandes scored the only goal in the third minute with a first-time volley. Sandro Tonali and Martin Dubravka also made their debuts in the behind-closed-doors friendly. Spurs will continue pre-season preparations to build chemistry ahead of the new campaign.

offbeat.title: How Crysencio Summerville's 'crazy 48 hours' unfolded as medical finally authorised for West Ham exit
offbeat.summary: Crysencio Summerville’s move to Saudi Pro League side Al-Hilal is set to be completed after West Ham accepted a £55 million fee with potential add-ons pushing the deal to £65 million. The 24-year-old Dutch winger has given the green light and will undergo a medical ahead of the transfer. West Ham’s total income from player exits this summer now exceeds £150 million following earlier sales. The move caps a frenetic 48 hours involving competing bids from AS Roma before Al-Hilal secured the deal.

offbeat.title: Kate Middleton se confie sur ce passe-temps qui l’a aidée à « s’évader » durant son combat contre le cancer
offbeat.summary: Kate Middleton a partagé son expérience de lutte contre le cancer, soulignant l'importance de maintenir des activités qui apportent du plaisir pendant la maladie. La princesse de Galles a notamment mentionné les difficultés cognitives qu'elle a rencontrées pendant son traitement, notamment la fatigue et la baisse de l'attention. Elle a également visité des patients à l'hôpital Christie pour leur apporter soutien et évoqué son propre combat contre le cancer. Kate Middleton a repris ses activités et engagements royaux après sa rémission en 2025.

offbeat.title: Forte dei Marmi, rissa in pieno centro nel locale storico: volano sedie e tavolini
offbeat.summary: A violent brawl erupted in Forte dei Marmi’s historic Caffè Morin around 1:30 a.m. Saturday, with chairs, tables and even a bicycle hurled in the chaos. Dozens of young people—many reportedly minors—clashed in the upscale Versilia resort, captured on phones but not reported to police. Authorities arrived to disperse the crowd as bystanders fled. Local officials cite rising late-night disturbances along viale Morin this summer.

offbeat.title: Soap legend with 'huge talent' returning to Coronation Street after 20 years
offbeat.summary: Coronation Street is welcoming back actress Elizabeth Rider, 68, after a 20-year absence. She will play a new role on the show this summer, marking her fourth appearance on the ITV soap. Rider, a legendary actress with a huge amount of talent, has had a long and impressive career in British television, including roles in EastEnders, Emmerdale, and Hollyoaks. Her return is expected to bring excitement to the show's upcoming storylines.

offbeat.title: Boyd found guilty of murdering Mark Carroll in Finglas
offbeat.summary: Derek Boyd, 31, was convicted of murdering Mark Carroll in Finglas, Dublin, on Wednesday after a trial at the Central Criminal Court. Boyd fatally stabbed Carroll in June 2024 during a row at his home while serving a suspended sentence for manslaughter. He will be sentenced on Tuesday to life in prison. Boyd fled to Scotland hours after the killing but was later arrested and extradited.

offbeat.title: Ed Harris Felt ‘Misled’ by ‘Dutton Ranch’ About ‘Inconsequential’ Role: ‘Get Me the F— Out of Here’ (EXCLUSIVE)
offbeat.summary: Veteran actor Ed Harris felt misled about his role on the "Yellowstone" spinoff "Dutton Ranch," stating he was told he'd be one of four main characters but felt underused and inconsequential. Harris expressed his discontent, saying "get me the f— out of here" halfway through filming Season 1. He's signed on for two seasons, with filming for Season 2 set to start in February 2027. Harris was reportedly told his character will have more to do in Season 2.

offbeat.title: Lucía Pombo y Álvaro López-Huertas ya son padres de su primera hija
offbeat.summary: Lucía Pombo and Álvaro López-Huertas have welcomed their first child, a daughter named Lola Belén, on July 21. The couple, who met through their parents' friendship, had been expecting the arrival of their baby after announcing the pregnancy in April. This marks a new chapter for the pair as they begin their journey as parents.

offbeat.title: Jamel Debbouze, comblé par Mélissa : "ne change absolument rien"
offbeat.summary: Jamel Debbouze celebrated his wife Mélissa Theuriau’s 48th birthday on July 18 with a heartfelt Instagram post. The couple, married since 2008, has two children. Debbouze wrote, "Continue to shine, make others shine, and light up our lives." The post, featuring a tender photo of the pair, resonated widely online.

offbeat.title: Reynolds and Mac share Wrexham's European excitement
offbeat.summary: Wrexham Women's team will make their European debut on Wednesday at the historic Racecourse Stadium, hosting Armenian side FC Pyunik in a Women's Champions League first qualifying round semi-final. This marks the first time Wrexham will play European football in 30 years, following the club's rapid rise under Hollywood owners Ryan Reynolds and Rob Mac. The team's head coach, Jenny Sugarman, says the owners are "super excited" for the team's progress. The match will be a significant step in Wrexham's ambition to become one of the world's top women's teams.

offbeat.title: Mercato : accord en vue entre l'AS Monaco et le FC Nantes pour Matthis Abline
offbeat.summary: AS Monaco is close to signing French forward Matthis Abline from FC Nantes for a record-breaking 30 million euros. Abline, 23, has already agreed to join the Principality. The transfer would be a significant deal for both clubs, with Abline set to become the most expensive player in FC Nantes' history. The deal is expected to be finalized soon, marking a major departure for FC Nantes after a season of relegation struggles.

offbeat.title: France pesticide fight : Macron rejects Monique Barbut’s resignation as farm law opens disputed exemptions
offbeat.summary: France’s President Emmanuel Macron rejected Environment Minister Monique Barbut’s resignation on July 22, 2026, after she opposed a new farm law allowing disputed pesticide exemptions. Parliament had passed the emergency agricultural legislation the day before, sparking her protest over reauthorizing acetamiprid and flupyradifurone. Barbut, who called the move an environmental setback, remains in post despite her resignation. The government now faces continued debate over pesticide policy and ecological standards.

offbeat.title: MLS schedule Wednesday, July 22: Will Lewandowski debut? Is Messi available?
offbeat.summary: Major League Soccer resumed its season Wednesday, July 22, with a full 15-match slate after a World Cup break. Inter Miami hosts Chicago Fire at 7:30 p.m. ET, with Robert Lewandowski’s MLS debut possible and Lionel Messi’s availability uncertain. All games stream on Apple TV, with Saturday’s 15 matches leading to the July 29 All-Star Game against Mexico’s Liga MX.

offbeat.title: Harry Potter keert voorlopig voor het laatst terug naar het grote doek: Pathé viert 25-jarig jubileum met 24-uurs marathon
offbeat.summary: Harry Potter films return to Dutch cinemas for a 25th anniversary celebration. From August 26 to September 2, Pathé will screen the first film at a reduced Classic rate and host a 24-hour marathon on August 29-30, showing all eight films. This is reportedly the last time the series will be shown in Dutch cinemas. Fans can purchase tickets starting July 31, Harry Potter's birthday.

offbeat.title: Violenta lite a Bari Sardo, ucciso un giovane
offbeat.summary: A 19-year-old man, Simone Concas, was killed in a violent altercation in Bari Sardo, Italy. He was stabbed repeatedly by the driver of a car after a dispute near a sports field at around 6 pm. A 25-year-old woman who was with him was also injured and taken to hospital in Cagliari. The incident is under investigation.

offbeat.title: Gatti sforzeschi, l'appello di Feltri al Tg4: "Ascoltate questi volontari. Andate loro incontro"
offbeat.summary: Eighteen cats at the Castello Sforzesco in Milan are in need of accessible entrances to receive daily care. The cats' colony has gained public attention through a campaign promoted by a local newspaper and supported by a television program. The city's mayor is being asked to find a solution to allow volunteers to continue assisting the cats. The situation is expected to be addressed further in the coming days.

offbeat.title: ‘The doctors will decide’ – Adam Yates may not continue in Tour de France due to stomach infection
offbeat.summary: British cyclist Adam Yates is struggling with a stomach infection at the 2026 Tour de France, finishing stage 17 over 26 minutes behind the winner. His participation in the final stages is uncertain, with crucial support needed for race leader Tadej Pogačar in the high mountains. Yates's condition must improve significantly for him to be of assistance. His team will decide on his continued participation in the coming days.

offbeat.title: Hoje pode ver em direto na TV…
offbeat.summary: Tonight’s live sports lineup includes SC Braga facing Zeleznicar Pancevo in the UEFA Conference League second qualifying round at 20:00. Levski Sofia and Universitatea Craiova kick off the Champions League second leg at 18:30. The Tour de France rolls on with Stage 17 from Chambéry to Voiron starting at 12:15. Brazilian league action continues with Coritiba against Palmeiras at 23:30.

offbeat.title: Spanish singer Rosalía sparks outrage in Argentina
offbeat.summary: Spanish singer Rosalía faced backlash in Argentina after a performance in Buenos Aires on March 15, where she wore a dress resembling a traditional Catholic garment. The controversy erupted when images of her outfit sparked accusations of cultural appropriation. Authorities and religious groups condemned the attire as disrespectful. The incident has drawn widespread attention on social media.

offbeat.title: Jasmine Crockett 'finds a racism underbelly in every single place': Martha MacCalum
offbeat.summary: Congresswoman Jasmine Crockett testified Tuesday at a House Oversight hearing on diversity, equity and inclusion in law schools, calling out what she described as systemic racism in legal education. The hearing, held in Washington, D.C., drew attention after Crockett’s remarks about encountering racial bias in multiple institutions. Lawmakers are expected to review testimony and consider policy recommendations in the coming weeks.

offbeat.title: Philipsen wins stage 17 and reopens a battle for the green jersey that seemed closed
offbeat.summary: Jasper Philipsen won stage 17 of the Tour de France 2026, a 174.7 kilometer race between Chambéry and Voiron. The Belgian rider from Alpecin-Premier Tech took the victory, with Mauro Schmid second and Olav Kooij third. Philipsen's win reopens the battle for the green jersey, closing in on leader Mads Pedersen. The favorites in the general classification remain unchanged, now facing the decisive alpine triptych.

offbeat.title: Dream School maakt doorstart met nieuwe gezichten
offbeat.summary: Dream School krijgt een doorstart met een nieuwe schoolleiding. Voetbalscheidsrechter Danny Makkelie wordt rector en Ouassima Tajmout mentor, vervangend Eric van ’t Zelfde en Lucia Rijker na zeven seizoenen. Het achtste seizoen verschijnt in 2027 op televisie. De nieuwe gezichten moeten jongeren opnieuw inspireren.

offbeat.title: Max Wöber leaves Leeds United
offbeat.summary: Austrian defender Max Wöber has left Leeds United after the club and player agreed to mutually terminate his contract. Wöber joined Leeds in 2023 from RB Salzburg and made 19 appearances in his debut season. He scored one goal in eight league appearances for the club in the 2024/25 season. Wöber now departs Elland Road on a permanent basis.

offbeat.title: Delmastro: the Executive Committee has rejected the prosecutors’ request to obtain the chat records. The M5S occupies the government benches
offbeat.summary: The Senate’s Committee on Authorisation to Proceed blocked prosecutors from accessing chat records between Deputy Andrea Delmastro and a detained restaurateur linked to mafia charges. Five Star Movement senators protested on the government benches, chanting against the decision, while centre-right MPs supported the refusal. The committee cited vague requests and lack of less intrusive alternatives. Prosecutors may now seek alternative legal avenues.

offbeat.title: Car plows into crowd in Modena, killing 55-year-old woman after two months of agony
offbeat.summary: A 55-year-old woman died today after being struck by a car in Modena’s city center two months ago. Monica Esposito, hit on May 16 while in a crowd, suffered critical injuries and never regained consciousness despite treatment. The driver, Salim El Koudri, remains under investigation as authorities assess the incident. Her husband, also injured in the crash, is recovering in a rehabilitation facility.

offbeat.title: Vijftien gewonden bij botsing trams op Erasmusbrug in Rotterdam, 'vreselijk ongeluk'
offbeat.summary: Two trams collided on Rotterdam’s Erasmus Bridge around 11:10 a.m., injuring 15 people, one seriously. Emergency crews freed a trapped passenger and treated others on-site or at hospitals. The crash damaged both trams and closed the bridge to all traffic. Authorities later adjusted the injury count from an earlier higher figure.

offbeat.title: Fakir Case: Lawyer Slams Authorities for Blaming Moroccan Man’s Murder on His Character
offbeat.summary: Italian police fatally shot Moroccan man Abderrahim Fakir outside Bologna on Sunday. Bologna-based asylum lawyer Barbara Spinelli condemned attempts to blame Fakir, calling him a worker who struggled after laying off employees due to declining business. Spinelli, who handled his residence permit renewal last year, emphasized his character over speculation. Authorities have not released further details on the shooting.

offbeat.title: Foyle Cup: Record-breaking tournament kicks off
offbeat.summary: A record 1,013 teams are participating in the largest Foyle Cup in the tournament's 34-year history, with almost 4,000 matches scheduled across over 60 venues in the north west. The six-day tournament kicked off on Monday with a traditional opening parade in Londonderry. Thousands of young footballers from Europe and North America are taking part. The event is expected to conclude on Saturday.

offbeat.title: | Comunicado Oficial | Información LVII Trofeo Colombino
offbeat.summary: Thousands of fans are expected to attend the LVII Trofeo Colombino at the Nuevo Colombino stadium in Huelva, Spain on Wednesday, prompting organizers to advise early arrival due to high expected attendance. The majority of parking lots near the stadium are closed for Fiestas Colombinas setup, except for a designated area near Gol Norte accessible from Avenida del Decano. Fans driving to the stadium should be prepared for potential traffic congestion. The event is set to take place on July 21, 2026.

offbeat.title: Mamdani backs off pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
offbeat.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has backed off his pledge to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, citing a lack of authority to do so. Mamdani now calls on US officials to act on the International Criminal Court's arrest warrant for Netanyahu over alleged war crimes. The US is not a member of the ICC, which would allow for Netanyahu's arrest in an ICC member state. Mamdani's reversal comes after President Donald Trump stated Netanyahu would not be arrested in the US.

offbeat.title: €1.5 billion for tax cuts in Budget - Economic Statement
offbeat.summary: The Irish government's Summer Economic Statement reveals €1.5 billion will be allocated for tax cuts in the upcoming Budget, set for October. This is part of a €8.5 billion package that includes €7 billion in spending increases, bringing total expenditure to €125 billion in 2027. The tax cuts are aimed at allowing workers to keep more of their pay, with the government committed to personal taxation changes. The statement is being published by Minister for Finance Simon Harris and Minister for Public Expenditure Jack Chambers.

offbeat.title: Bohs look to lay Dalymount platform against Ballkani
offbeat.summary: Bohemians face Ballkani in the Conference League second round qualifier at Dalymount Park. Bohemians aim to build on their first European hurdle success, having secured a minimum of €700,000 in prize money. They previously defeated St Joseph's 2-0 and drew goalless in the first qualifying round. The winner will advance to the next round, with a sterner test expected against Ballkani.

offbeat.title: Delmastro, Vannacci e la fine della questione morale in Italia
offbeat.summary: Italian politician Delmastro has been granted parliamentary immunity, blocking magistrates from investigating his business dealings. The move has sparked widespread criticism and protests, with a demonstration in Bologna two days ago. The immunity will hinder investigations into Delmastro's family business and a high-profile case involving a former justice minister. The outcome is likely to fuel further controversy and public outcry.

offbeat.title: Man Utd news: £60m transfer target makes decision as star gets 'calls and texts' over move
offbeat.summary: Manchester United striker Joshua Zirkzee is reportedly a transfer target for Italian club Juventus. The Dutch forward has struggled to make an impact in the Premier League since joining United in 2024 for £36.5 million. Zirkzee has scored just five goals in 56 Premier League appearances. Juventus is considering a move for the 23-year-old forward.

offbeat.title: 1win y FUTTO establecen el récord mundial oficial del partido de fútbol disputado a mayor altura sobre un edificio
offbeat.summary: A record-breaking 3-on-3 soccer match was held at 196 meters above Bogotá, Colombia, on a helipad atop the Torre Colpatria building. The event, organized by 1win and FUTTO, was certified by World Record Certification as the highest soccer match ever played. Six Colombian soccer influencers participated in the match, which was part of a larger celebration of soccer culture. The event's success is expected to be followed by more innovative soccer experiences.

offbeat.title: VNL Finals: Brasile-Giappone 3-1, l’Italia incrocia le verdeoro in semifinale
offbeat.summary: In the VNL Finals, Brazil defeated Japan 3-1, advancing to the semifinals where they will face Italy on Saturday, July 25, at the East Asian Games in Macao. Italy had previously defeated the Netherlands 3-0 and will be looking to avenge their previous loss to Brazil in the first week of the intercontinental phase. The winner of the Italy-Brazil match will face the winner of the Turkey-Canada or USA-China match in the final.
offbeat.trend: nazionale femminile di pallavolo del brasile

offbeat.title: The Walking Dead: Dead City Season 3 Is a Welcome Sort-of-Reboot That Puts Maggie and Negan on the Right Road
offbeat.summary: The Walking Dead: Dead City Season 3 premieres Sunday, July 26 at 9pm ET/PT on AMC and AMC+, marking a new direction for the series with Maggie and Negan as frenemies-to-friends. The third season delivers compelling new elements and a bold new direction, a welcome change from previous seasons. With a messy premiere episode, the series finds its footing in the back half, culminating in a standout episode. The new path for the characters is a promising start to the season.

offbeat.title: Las redes sociales de À Punt superan el millón y medio de seguidores
offbeat.summary: À Punt's social media presence has surpassed 1.5 million followers across various platforms. The public Valencian television network's profiles on Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook have grown by 32% over the past year, with a total increase of over 230,000 followers. À Punt Mèdia's Facebook channel leads with over 269,000 followers, followed by its Instagram channel with 228,000. This growth is accompanied by a 45% increase in digital content production in the first half of 2026.

offbeat.title: Israel may equip IDF with anti-submarine aircraft as tensions with Turkey grow
offbeat.summary: Israel is considering purchasing Boeing P-8 Poseidon anti-submarine aircraft for the IDF amid rising tensions with Turkey. The P-8 Poseidon, based on a converted commercial jet, has a 2,200-kilometer operational range and can detect diesel submarines using advanced sensors. The aircraft carries a nine-member crew and can carry torpedoes, bombs, and naval mines. The IDF currently uses the Oron for strategic intelligence and surveillance.

offbeat.title: Garnacho, Rogers and two clubs trying to balance the books
offbeat.summary: Chelsea's financial woes persist despite a record £300m in player sales last season, with the club still carrying significant debts. They were fined £2.6m by Uefa for breaching financial regulations, but can write off £1.7m if they meet targets by next summer. Chelsea's squad value is £1.3bn, second only to Manchester City in the Premier League. The club will continue to navigate Uefa's financial rules as they balance their books.

offbeat.title: Dublin Airport is about to boom. Why is Aer Lingus shrinking itself?
offbeat.summary: Aer Lingus is cutting 7.7% of its flights and staff, despite Dublin Airport's passenger cap being lifted and charges reduced. This move comes as the airline's parent company, International Airlines Group, prioritizes operating margins across its subsidiaries. IAG's focus on 12-15% margins determines investment allocation, with airlines meeting this target receiving funding for new planes and routes. The decision may raise questions about Aer Lingus' place within IAG, following a €1.4 billion acquisition in 2015.

offbeat.title: Başakşehir vs Inter Turku match lineup | UEFA Europa Conference League | Qualification · 22 Jul 2026
offbeat.summary: Başakşehir and Inter Turku faced off in the UEFA Europa Conference League Qualification on Wednesday, with Başakşehir leading in a 4-4-2 formation under head coach Nuri Şahin. The match was played at Basaksehir Fatih Terim Stadyumu. The teams' lineups and formations were key to the match, with Inter Turku setting up in a 4-3-3. The outcome of the match is not specified in the provided reports.

offbeat.title: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
offbeat.summary: Two Russians were sentenced to 11 and eight years in prison in Angola’s capital, Luanda, for terrorism and spying. Igor Ratchin and Lev Lakshtanov were convicted over alleged efforts to incite protests, spread disinformation and interfere in next year’s presidential election. Authorities linked them to Africa Politology, described as a Russian political network tied to the Wagner group. Both denied involvement and charges.

offbeat.title: Geld versteckt: Böhmermann-Zuschauer haben "die Möglichkeit, richtig reich zu werden"
offbeat.summary: Jan Böhmermann has hidden 900 euros in nine secret locations in and around Kassel, Germany. Viewers of his ZDF show "Lass dich überwachen!" have the chance to find the money. The locations are revealed in a recent episode featuring a local resident and geocacher, Bernd.

offbeat.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
offbeat.summary: A woman in France has been formally charged in the 2024 stabbing death of British-South African mother Karen Carter, 65, in the village of Trémolat. The 70-year-old suspect was initially held in 2025 before release and now faces judicial inquiry after prosecutors cited new evidence. Carter, who managed holiday rentals, was found near her car with fatal wounds. Authorities continue investigating the motive as her grieving husband describes the family’s shock.

offbeat.title: The PSOE backs Zapatero and leaves his future in the hands of Justice: "The truth will come to light"
offbeat.summary: The PSOE reaffirmed its full support for former Spanish Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero on Wednesday amid new allegations tied to the Plus Ultra rescue operation. The party called for the ongoing judicial investigation to clarify the facts, stating no reason exists to doubt Zapatero’s account. PSOE spokesperson Montse Mínguez emphasized that courts will determine the truth as the case remains in the investigative phase. The party maintained its confidence in Zapatero, asserting all his actions were lawful.

offbeat.title: Obituary for David J. Ascione
offbeat.summary: David J. Ascione, known as D.J., died July 19, 2026, at 62 in Toms River, New Jersey. A lifelong resident, he worked at Ascione Florist and Greenhouse on Route 9 and served with the Pleasant Plains Volunteer Fire Company. He is survived by his mother, sister, aunt, uncle, and extended family across New Jersey and Arizona. Funeral services will be private, with flowers or memorial trees accepted at the family’s floral shop.
offbeat.sourceName: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals
sources: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals

politics.title: Scottish Labour’s rivals decry ‘chancer’ Anas Sarwar for resigning to join Andy Burnham’s government
politics.summary: Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar resigned to join Andy Burnham’s government as a peer and trade minister. Rivals in Holyrood accused him of hypocrisy for accepting a House of Lords seat after previously vowing to abolish it. The SNP demanded he donate his salary to charity, calling his move unacceptable. Labour has not commented on the criticism.

politics.title: ‘He knows what he’s doing’: Burnham’s off-the-cuff remarks inspire headlines and headaches
politics.summary: British Prime Minister Andy Burnham is facing controversy over his handling of policy changes, including a possible income tax threshold increase, just three days into his tenure. He has been accused of performing U-turns on key issues, including taxes for higher earners and private sector rents. The income tax threshold change is a major point of contention, with reports suggesting it may be raised. Burnham's unscripted comments are sparking headaches for his administration.

politics.title: Natalie Fleet leaves 'triggering' safeguarding minister role
politics.summary: Labour MP Natalie Fleet resigned as safeguarding minister on 12 May this year after finding the role overwhelmingly triggering. The former victim of grooming and rape cited the job’s exposure to harrowing cases as making her ill. She announced her departure on X, calling it the bravest decision she had made. Fleet will support her successor in the role.

entertainment.title: FCC Chair Says ABC’s Decision to Not Broadcast Trump Speech Live Will Be Part of Agency’s Review of Station Licenses
entertainment.summary: FCC Chair Brendan Carr says ABC’s decision to skip live coverage of Donald Trump’s July 16 speech on election security will be reviewed as part of the agency’s early license checks for eight ABC-owned stations. Trump claimed during the speech that China illegally accessed millions of voter files to manipulate U.S. elections in 2018 and 2020. Carr, a Trump appointee, has previously argued broadcasters may be required to air presidential remarks live. The FCC’s review could determine whether the stations face penalties or license adjustments.

entertainment.title: European Commission Approves Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger
entertainment.summary: The European Commission approved the $111 billion merger between Paramount Skydance and Warner Bros. Discovery on Wednesday, removing a major hurdle for the deal. As a condition, Paramount must end its European film distribution partnership with Universal within 13 months. A U.S. federal judge temporarily paused the merger on Monday following a request from 12 states citing competition concerns. The commission concluded the deal won’t harm competition in TV or film production, though it flagged film distribution concentration risks.

entertainment.title: Sophie Thatcher Is Ready to Move Beyond ‘Yellowjackets’ and Step Out of the Darkness
entertainment.summary: Sophie Thatcher, known for her dark roles in Yellowjackets and Heretic, is shifting gears with a new project. The 22-year-old actor, raised in a Mormon household, is stepping away from intense characters to explore lighter roles. Her next film, Companion, marks a departure from her previous work. Critics expect her to bring fresh energy to upcoming projects.

sports.title: Rangers to fight McInnes ban for 'disgusting' penalty remark
sports.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been handed a four-match touchline ban by the Scottish FA for calling a late penalty decision "disgusting" in May. The penalty, awarded to Celtic's Kelechi Iheanacho, was a stoppage-time spot-kick that kept Celtic one point behind Hearts, who ultimately won the title. McInnes will contest the ban, stating the remarks were directed at the decision, not officials. He will be in the stands as Rangers open the new Scottish Premiership season.

sports.title: McInnes given four-match ban for 'disgusting' Celtic penalty remark
sports.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been given a four-match ban by the Scottish FA for his "disgusting" comments about a late penalty awarded to Celtic at Motherwell in May. The penalty, converted by Kelechi Iheanacho, helped Celtic stay one point behind Hearts, who ultimately won the title. McInnes will serve the ban, with one match suspended until the end of the season. He will be in the stands as Rangers open their Scottish Premiership campaign.

sports.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
sports.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after signing Brazilian midfielder Casemiro. The league confirmed the probe Wednesday, the same day Miami announced his three-year deal with an option to extend through June 2029. Inter Miami claims it acquired Casemiro’s discovery rights from the LA Galaxy, though the league has not yet released settlement terms. The investigation focuses on whether Miami followed MLS rules in securing his rights.

breaking.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
breaking.summary: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein, Daniel Siad, 69, was found dead at his home in Colombes, a Paris suburb, on Monday. An investigation into his death has been opened, with a post-mortem examination to be carried out. Siad's name appeared thousands of times in Epstein files, and he had been under investigation for organised human trafficking and conspiracy charges since February. The investigation will continue to identify all individuals potentially involved.

breaking.title: Daniel Siad, Model Scout Who Connected Women to Epstein, Is Found Dead in France
breaking.summary: Daniel Siad, who was found dead on Monday, repeatedly introduced Jeffrey Epstein to young women, according to messages he exchanged with Mr. Epstein. Mr. Siad had denied wrongdoing.

breaking.title: Hitler’s Birth Place in Austria Is Now a Police Station
breaking.summary: The government repurposed the 17th century building in hopes of preventing the site from becoming an attraction for Nazi sympathizers.

facts.title: Robotic Servicing Mission Launches with NASA Support
facts.summary: A robotic spacecraft, the Mission Robotic Vehicle, has launched from Cape Canaveral on a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket and is now en route to geosynchronous Earth orbit. The NASA-supported Robotic Servicing of Geosynchronous Satellites payload will use its advanced robotics to service spacecraft, extending the life of existing satellites by installing propulsion modules. Funded by DARPA, the mission aims to advance US capabilities for in-space servicing and assembly. The spacecraft is expected to begin inspecting and upgrading satellites in orbit.

crisis.title: Aston Martin secures £550m loan deal
crisis.summary: Aston Martin has secured a £550m loan deal to bolster its finances. The luxury car maker, headquartered in Gaydon, Warwickshire, has been struggling with net losses of £493.2m last year, a 50% increase. The loan will fund future product plans and bolster its balance sheet. The company's half-year results are set to be published on 29 July.

crisis.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
crisis.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

health.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
health.summary: A parliamentary report urges Britain to target ultra-processed foods to cut obesity rates. MPs highlight sugar and refined carbs as key drivers, not fat, citing health risks like type 2 diabetes. Current policies still focus on fat reduction, pushing manufacturers to replace it with sweeteners. The call comes as obesity costs the NHS billions annually, with reformulation targets set for next year.

health.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
health.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City’s legionnaires’ disease outbreak, bringing the toll to five fatalities among 82 confirmed cases. Health officials traced the outbreak to cooling towers in Manhattan’s Upper East Side, where 34 tested positive for legionella bacteria. No new cases have been reported in six days, but officials warn figures may change as testing continues. Remaining cooling tower results are expected within a week.

health.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
health.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

tech.title: Lawsuit Against Meta Over Social Media Addiction Is Dropped
tech.summary: For Meta, the move was a reprieve after it and YouTube were found guilty in another case in March of negligence and personal injury for their platforms’ addictive features.

tech.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
tech.summary: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

tech.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Hacked Into Hugging Face, a Digital Library
tech.summary: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.
